turns.content: Hey, I'm having trouble wrapping my mind around when to actually use a LinkedList over a Vec or VecDeque. I saw this code in a blog post where someone used std::collections::LinkedList and accessed elements with cursor_front instead of indexing like you'd do with a Vec.

That's a great question, and the analogy is easiest to think of it as choosing between a bookshelf (Vec) and a conveyor belt (LinkedList). A VecDeque lets you grab items from either end quickly but you still have to scan through everything to find something in the middle. But with cursor_mut on a LinkedList, inserting or removing an item at any position takes constant time regardless of where it is.
Got it, so if I need random access most of the time, VecDeque still wins?

Exactly. If you're doing frequent middle edits or deletions, the LinkedList shines because of cursor_mut avoiding that linear scan overhead. But just to be sure we're aligned on your use case: are you expecting random access operations like get_index(i) frequently in your code?
